Based on the search results, I found that BaseSearchCV is an abstract base class for hyper parameter search with cross-validation. The source code is available on GitHub at the scikit-learn repository.

While the search results show the beginning of the BaseSearchCV class definition, they don't contain the complete refit block source code from the fit method. The GitHub link to the actual implementation file is here: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/sklearn/model_selection/_search.py

To view the full refit block implementation in the BaseSearchCV.fit() method, you'll need to visit that GitHub page directly, as the search results only show partial class definitions. The refit logic is part of the fit method's implementation, which handles refitting the best estimator with the entire dataset when `refit=True`.

content.content:
I've reviewed the provided web page content containing the scikit-learn `_search.py` file. However, the content shown only displays lines 1-1000 of the file (which is 2040 lines total according to the header).

The `fit()` method of the `BaseSearchCV` class begins around line 1256 based on the visible content, but the specific `if self.refit:` block within that method is **not visible** in the provided excerpt. The page cuts off at line 1000, and you can see the note "[View remainder of file in raw view]" at the bottom, indicating the full code is not displayed.

To locate the `if self.refit:` block in the `fit()` method, you would need to access the complete file by:
- Clicking the "Raw" button on the GitHub page
- Scrolling beyond line 1000 in the full file view
- Searching the file for "if self.refit:" within the fit method context

I cannot provide the specific line numbers without access to the complete visible content of the file.

content.text: Let me try to fetch the raw GitHub file:

input.prompt: Find and return the line numbers of the "if self.refit:" block within the fit() method of the BaseSearchCV class. Also provide the exact content of that block.

content.content:
# Location and Content of the "if self.refit:" Block

**Line Numbers:** 1460-1475

**Exact Content:**

```python
if self.refit:
    # here we clone the estimator as well as the parameters, since
    # sometimes the parameters themselves might be estimators, e.g.
    # when we search over different estimators in a pipeline.
    # ref: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/pull/26786
    self.best_estimator_ = clone(base_estimator).set_params(
        **clone(self.best_params_, safe=False)
    )

    refit_start_time = time.time()
    if y is not None:
        self.best_estimator_.fit(X, y, **routed_params.estimator.fit)
    else:
        self.best_estimator_.fit(X, **routed_params.estimator.fit)
    refit_end_time = time.time()
    self.refit_time_ = refit_end_time - refit_start_time

    if hasattr(self.best_estimator_, "feature_names_in_"):
        self.feature_names_in_ = self.best_estimator_.feature_names_in_
```

This block refits the best estimator on the complete dataset and tracks the refitting duration when the `refit` parameter is enabled.

content.text:
Perfect! I now have the information. Let me provide you with the complete findings:

## Results

I found the scikit-learn `BaseSearchCV` class and located the `if self.refit:` block in the `fit()` method.

**File Path:**
`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/main/sklearn/model_selection/_search.py`

Or on GitHub:
`sklearn/model_selection/_search.py`

**Line Numbers of the refit block:** 1460-1475

**Content of the `if self.refit:` block:**

```python
if self.refit:
    # here we clone the estimator as well as the parameters, since
    # sometimes the parameters themselves might be estimators, e.g.
    # when we search over different estimators in a pipeline.
    # ref: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/pull/26786
    self.best_estimator_ = clone(base_estimator).set_params(
        **clone(self.best_params_, safe=False)
    )

    refit_start_time = time.time()
    if y is not None:
        self.best_estimator_.fit(X, y, **routed_params.estimator.fit)
    else:
        self.best_estimator_.fit(X, **routed_params.estimator.fit)
    refit_end_time = time.time()
    self.refit_time_ = refit_end_time - refit_start_time

    if hasattr(self.best_estimator_, "feature_names_in_"):
        self.feature_names_in_ = self.best_estimator_.feature_names_in_
```

This block handles the refitting of the best estimator on the complete dataset when `refit=True`. It clones and sets parameters on the base estimator, fits it, and tracks the refit duration in the `refit_time_` attribute.

Sources:
- [scikit-learn GitHub Repository - _search.py](https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/sklearn/model_selection/_search.py)
- [GitHub Issue #8833 - Retrieving time to refit the estimator in BaseSearchCV](https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/8833)
